summary: Rear-end collision at intersection of Oak St and 5th Ave. Claimant stopped at red light, struck from behind by uninsured driver. Airbags deployed. Claimant transported to ER by ambulance.
policy_excerpt: Comprehensive auto policy, $500 deductible, $100K/$300K bodily injury, uninsured motorist coverage included. Policy effective 2025-09-01 through 2026-09-01.
incident_description: At approximately 3:45 PM on March 15, 2026, claimant's 2023 Honda Accord was stopped at a red light at the intersection of Oak Street and 5th Avenue. A 2019 Ford F-150 traveling at approximately 35 mph failed to stop and struck the claimant's vehicle from behind. Police report #26-04821 filed. Other driver had no valid insurance. Claimant reported neck pain, back pain, and headache. Transported to Memorial Hospital ER.
adjuster_memo: Vehicle damage estimated at $12,400. Claimant's medical bills to date: $4,200 (ER visit, imaging). Claimant reports ongoing neck stiffness and has started physical therapy. Uninsured motorist claim applies. Recommend reserve of $25,000.

summary: Water damage to finished basement from burst pipe during cold snap. Flooring, drywall, and personal property damaged.
policy_excerpt: Homeowners HO-3 policy, $1,000 deductible, dwelling coverage $450K, personal property $180K. Water damage from sudden and accidental discharge covered. Gradual seepage excluded.
incident_description: During the cold snap on March 10, 2026, temperatures dropped to -8F. A copper supply pipe running through an exterior wall in the basement burst, releasing water for approximately 4 hours before the homeowner discovered the leak upon returning from work. Water saturated approximately 600 sq ft of finished basement including engineered hardwood flooring, drywall to 3 feet, and damaged a home entertainment system, bookshelves, and stored items.
adjuster_memo: Pipe burst appears sudden and accidental, not gradual deterioration. Homeowner had heat set to 62F which is reasonable. No prior pipe claims. Mitigation company deployed same day. Estimate: flooring $8,200, drywall $3,400, contents $6,100, mitigation $2,800. Total: $20,500 less $1,000 deductible = $19,500 recommended payout.

summary: Third-party slip-and-fall at catered corporate event. Guest alleges wet floor near buffet station caused fall resulting in broken wrist.
policy_excerpt: Commercial general liability, $2M per occurrence, $4M aggregate. Products-completed operations included. Liquor liability excluded. Defense costs outside limits.
incident_description: On February 28, 2026, during a corporate holiday party catered by Riverside Catering at the Marriott Downtown Convention Center, a guest (David Chen, age 52) slipped on a wet area near the buffet station and fell, sustaining a fracture to his left wrist. Mr. Chen alleges the wet condition was caused by a spilled beverage that catering staff failed to clean up. Event had approximately 200 attendees. Incident witnessed by 3 people. No incident report filed by venue.
adjuster_memo: Claimant's attorney (Williams & Associates) sent demand letter for $175,000 including medical ($22,000 surgery + PT), lost wages ($18,000 — self-employed consultant), and pain/suffering. No venue incident report is concerning for our defense. Need to interview catering staff about cleanup protocols. Reservation of rights letter recommended given potential contributory negligence. Recommend reserve of $85,000.

summary: Pre-authorization request for elective knee replacement surgery. Claimant has documented 18 months of conservative treatment failure.
policy_excerpt: Group health PPO plan, $2,500 individual deductible (met), 80/20 coinsurance after deductible, $5,000 out-of-pocket max. Knee replacement surgery covered when medically necessary with prior authorization. Requires documented failure of conservative treatment for minimum 6 months.
incident_description: Maria Torres, age 58, is requesting pre-authorization for total knee arthroplasty (CPT 27447) on her right knee. She has been treated for severe osteoarthritis (ICD-10: M17.11) since September 2024. Conservative treatments attempted include: physical therapy (3 rounds, 36 sessions total), cortisone injections (4 rounds), hyaluronic acid injections (2 rounds), NSAIDs, and a hinged knee brace. Most recent MRI (February 2026) shows bone-on-bone contact with significant joint space narrowing. Orthopedist Dr. Robert Kim recommends surgical intervention.
adjuster_memo: Clinical review confirms 18 months of documented conservative treatment failure. BMI is 31.2 — within acceptable range for surgery (threshold is 40). No contraindications identified. Procedure estimated at $48,000 (facility + surgeon + anesthesia + implant). Network facility (St. Mary's Medical Center). Recommend approval with standard pre-op requirements.

summary: Single-vehicle accident, driver struck a deer on rural highway at dusk. Moderate front-end damage. No injuries reported.
policy_excerpt: Full coverage auto policy, $250 comprehensive deductible, $500 collision deductible. Comprehensive covers animal strikes. Glass coverage included. Rental car coverage: $40/day, 30-day max.
incident_description: On March 22, 2026 at approximately 6:30 PM, Robert Nguyen was driving his 2024 Toyota RAV4 northbound on County Road 47 near mile marker 12 when a deer ran into the road from the east shoulder. Driver attempted to brake but struck the deer at approximately 45 mph. Damage to front bumper, hood, right headlight assembly, and radiator support. Vehicle is drivable but headlight is non-functional. No police report filed. Deer carcass confirmed at scene by tow driver. No injuries to driver or passengers.
adjuster_memo: Straightforward comprehensive claim. Animal strike confirmed by tow driver statement and physical evidence (deer hair on bumper). Repair estimate from certified shop: $6,800. No rental needed — second vehicle in household. No prior claims on policy. Recommend payment of $6,550 ($6,800 less $250 comprehensive deductible).

summary: Roof damage from winter storm. Late filing — 72 days after loss. Claimant states she only noticed interior water stains last week and traced them to roof damage.
policy_excerpt: Homeowners HO-3 policy, $2,500 deductible, dwelling coverage $320K. Wind/hail damage covered. Claims must be reported promptly. Policy states: 'You must notify us of a loss as soon as reasonably possible.' Late reporting may affect coverage.
incident_description: Linda Washington reports that a winter storm on January 15, 2026 caused damage to her roof shingles. She states she did not notice the damage because it is on the rear slope of the roof not visible from ground level. On March 25, she noticed water stains on her bedroom ceiling and hired a roofer who found 15-20 missing or damaged shingles in a pattern consistent with wind damage. The roofer estimated the storm occurred 2-3 months prior based on weathering of exposed underlayment.
adjuster_memo: LATE FILING FLAG: 72 days between date of loss and reporting. Prompt notice clause may be relevant. However, claimant's explanation (not visible from ground) is plausible for rear-slope damage. Weather records confirm severe winds (60+ mph gusts) on 1/15/2026 in claimant's area. Concern: delay may have worsened interior water damage beyond original storm damage. Recommend field inspection to distinguish storm damage from deterioration/delayed-reporting damage. Estimate pending inspection.

summary: FRAUD INDICATORS: Claimant reports vehicle theft from gym parking lot. Third theft claim in 18 months across two policies. Vehicle found burned 2 days later.
policy_excerpt: Full coverage auto policy, $500 comprehensive deductible. Theft coverage included. Total loss threshold: repair cost exceeds 75% of ACV.
incident_description: Kevin O'Brien reports his 2022 BMW 330i was stolen from the parking lot of Ironworks Gym between 6:00 PM and 7:30 PM on March 25, 2026. Claimant states he parked in an unmonitored area of the lot. Vehicle was recovered on March 27, 2026 in a rural area approximately 30 miles away, burned beyond repair. Police report #26-05291 filed. Vehicle ACV estimated at $34,000.
adjuster_memo: SIU REFERRAL RECOMMENDED. Multiple red flags: (1) Third vehicle theft/total-loss claim in 18 months — previous claims on Policy AUTO-4412908 ($28K) and current policy ($22K); (2) Vehicle found burned, eliminating physical evidence; (3) Gym parking lot has CCTV but claimant parked in only unmonitored section; (4) Claimant recently added comprehensive coverage 45 days before loss — previously had liability-only; (5) Claimant has two active auto loans with high monthly payments. Recommend SIU investigation before any payment. Reserve $34,000 pending outcome.

summary: Warehouse worker reports lower back injury from lifting heavy box. Employer disputes account of events.
policy_excerpt: Workers' compensation policy, statutory limits per state law. Medical benefits: unlimited reasonable and necessary treatment. Temporary total disability: 66.67% of average weekly wage, $980 max/week. Employer has modified duty program available.
incident_description: Anthony Reeves, age 34, warehouse associate at MidWest Distribution Center, reports he injured his lower back while lifting a 75-lb box from floor level at approximately 2:15 PM on March 18, 2026. He states he felt a sharp pain and was unable to continue working. He was seen at the company's occupational health clinic the same day. Diagnosis: lumbar strain (ICD-10: S39.012A). Restrictions: no lifting over 10 lbs, no bending/twisting, for 2 weeks. Employer states no witness observed the injury and notes the claimant had requested a shift change to days earlier that week, which was denied.
adjuster_memo: Claim accepted provisionally. Medical treatment appears reasonable. Employer's dispute noted but does not meet threshold for denial — unwitnessed injuries are common in warehouse settings. Modified duty available: light administrative tasks at same hourly rate. If claimant accepts modified duty, no lost-time benefits owed. If claimant cannot perform modified duty per medical restrictions, TTD benefits at $653/week (66.67% of $980 AWW). Recommend 3-point contact (claimant, employer, treating physician) within 48 hours. Estimated reserve: $8,500 (medical) + $5,200 (4 weeks TTD if needed) = $13,700.
